concerns.text: Task T011 instructs downloading data from 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/numenta/NAB/master/data/...' (an anomaly detection benchmark) instead of the American Gut Project or Open Humans sources mandated by FR-001 and the Plan. This is a silent relaxation of the data source constraint, violating the requirement to use specific public datasets for the study.

Phase 6 (Tasks T037-T042) introduces 'molecular binding affinity estimation' and 'structural interaction models' which are not authorized by any Functional Requirement (FR) or Success Criterion (SC) in the spec. The spec explicitly limits the scope to statistical association (FR-003, FR-004, FR-008). This task set silently expands the scope beyond the constitutional and spec-defined boundaries, introducing unverified causal modeling attempts.

Task T024 implements the 'diet type' substitution for 'diet timing' (as noted in the Plan) but fails to explicitly document this deviation from FR-004 in the task description. This obscures the traceability gap between the spec's explicit requirement and the implemented solution, violating the 'Single Source of Truth' principle regarding requirement traceability.

Task T040 requires implementing a 'Molecular Limitation' section stating the study 'remains at statistical association level'. While the content is correct, the task framing risks conflating the study's design limitation with a failure condition. The spec (US-3, FR-008) requires the *report* to frame findings as associational, not the code to enforce a 'limitation' flag that might be misinterpreted as a study failure rather than a methodological boundary defined in the Plan.
